title: TakeoffItem
description: TakeoffItem: Class storing takeoff values of relevant quantities corresponding to a particular material.

Material.description: Material to which the takeoff values correspond. Material: Physical material to be used for Takeoffs and asigned to Physical elements. Material is capable of storing discipline specific data in Properties.

Volume.description: Total volume of the material. Applicable for most takeoffs. Property has a quantity of type Volume measured in [m³].

Mass.description: Total mass of the material. Applicable for materials with a set density. Property has a quantity of type Mass measured in [kg].

Area.description: Total area of the material. Applicable for takeoffs of 2-dimensional elements or 2-dimensional parts. Property has a quantity of type Area measured in [m²].

Length.description: Total length of the material. Applicable for takeoffs of 1-dimensional elements or elements with 1-dimensional parts. Property has a quantity of type Length measured in [m].

NumberItem.description: Total number of items containing the material in the takeoff. Applicable for most takeoffs.

ElectricCurrent.description: Total Electric current associated with the material. Applicable for takeoffs of some electrical equipment elements. Property has a quantity of type ElectricCurrent measured in [A].

Energy.description: Total Electric current associated with the material. Applicable for takeoffs of some electrical equipment elements. Property has a quantity of type Energy measured in [J].

Power.description: Total Power or apparent power associated with the material. Applicable for takeoffs of some electrical equipment elements. Property has a quantity of type EnergyPerUnitTime measured in [W].

VolumetricFlowRate.description: Total VolumetricFlowRate associated with the material. Applicable for takeoffs of elements relating to flow of substance through the element. Property has a quantity of type VolumetricFlowRate measured in [m³/s].

_bhomVersion.description: Optional version of BHoM used as part of automatic versioning and schema upgrades.
